channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Michigan muscles its way to program's 2nd NCAA basketball title, beating UConn
item.description: High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.
item.content:encoded:
High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.

(Image credit: Michael Conroy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What can Artemis II astronauts see that satellites haven't captured?
item.description: The astronauts on Artemis II will observe parts of the moon rarely seen by human eyes. A NASA planetary scientist said it will offer a vital perspective for lunar research.
item.content:encoded:
The astronauts on Artemis II will observe parts of the moon rarely seen by human eyes. A NASA planetary scientist said it will offer a vital perspective for lunar research.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Even when Arsenio Hall's show was a hit, 'everyone wanted it to be something else'
item.description: Hall's late-night show gave hip-hop a home on TV and helped propel Bill Clinton to the White House. "I wanted to do this show that didn't exist when I was a kid," he says. Hall's memoir is Arsenio.
item.content:encoded:
Hall's late-night show gave hip-hop a home on TV and helped propel Bill Clinton to the White House. "I wanted to do this show that didn't exist when I was a kid," he says. Hall's memoir is Arsenio.

(Image credit: Kevin Winter)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Medical supplies are stuck in Dubai, as clinics around the world face shortages
item.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

(Image credit: ‎)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
item.description: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.
item.content:encoded:
The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Over-the-counter medication abortion? These researchers say it would be safe
item.description: A paper in JAMA Internal Medicine adds to the growing scientific evidence that medication abortion pills would be safe to sell over-the-counter at the pharmacy. But political opposition means that possibility may not happen anytime soon.
item.content:encoded:
A paper in JAMA Internal Medicine adds to the growing scientific evidence that medication abortion pills would be safe to sell over-the-counter at the pharmacy. But political opposition means that possibility may not happen anytime soon.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
item.description: Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.
item.content:encoded:
Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
item.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: She paid into Medicare for years. Trump's immigration policy will end her coverage
item.description: A provision in the GOP's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will make Rosa María Carranza and an estimated 100,000 other lawfully present immigrant seniors ineligible. Her once secure retirement is in question.
item.content:encoded:
A provision in the GOP's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will make Rosa María Carranza and an estimated 100,000 other lawfully present immigrant seniors ineligible. Her once secure retirement is in question.

(Image credit: Hiram Alejandro Durán)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa G. Sánchez

item.title: Shingles can hit younger than you think. The vaccine can prevent the painful illness
item.description: A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.

(Image credit: triocean/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Milne-Tyte
